perturbation.original_text: All information which is disclosed by one party to the other in connection with discussions relating to the Transactions, whether before or after the date of execution of this Agreement, shall automatically be deemed proprietary or confidential and subject to this Agreement unless otherwise confirmed in writing by the disclosing party.
perturbation.changed_text: All data which is disclosed by one party to the other in connection with discussions relating to the Transactions, whether before or after the date of execution of this Agreement, shall automatically be deemed proprietary or confidential and subject to this Agreement unless otherwise confirmed in writing by the disclosing party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'information' to 'data'. The term 'Information' is defined in section 1, making the term 'data' undefined. This contradiction is present on whether the definition applies to 'data', introducing uncertainty. This is an in-text contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: In connection with ongoing discussions between Broker and Company concerning possible transactions (the "Transactions"), each party to this Agreement may wish to disclose certain proprietary and confidential information to the other party on a confidential basis. Such proprietary or confidential information ("Information") includes any and all technical and non-technical information, including without limitation, information concerning financial, accounting or marketing reports, business plans, analysis, forecasts, predictors, intellectual property, trade secrets and know-how disclosed in connection with the Transactions. "Information" may take the form of documentation, drawings, specifications, software, technical or engineering data and other forms, and may be communicated orally, in writing, by electronic or magnetic media, by visual observation and by other means. "Information" includes any reports, analysis, studies or other material, whether prepared by the receiving party or otherwise, that contains or are based upon proprietary or confidential information covered by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses the term 'information' which is defined in the 'Contradicted Text'. The changed text replaces 'information' with 'data'. Because 'data' is not defined, and 'information' is already specifically defined, this creates an in-text contradiction. The 'Contradicted Text' references 'Information', which is now potentially different from 'data' in the 'Changed Text', leading to confusion and uncertainty about what is subject to the agreement. The definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction applies here, as multiple terms are used without proper definition.

perturbation.original_text: With respect to information disclosed under this Agreement, the party to whom the information is disclosed and its Representatives shall: a. hold the information in confidence, exercising a degree of care not less than the care used by such party to protect its own proprietary or confidential information that it does not wish to disclose, and in no event less than a reasonable degree of care; b. restrict disclosure of the information solely to those Representatives with a need to know and not disclose it to any other person; c. advise those Representatives of their obligations with respect to the information; and d. use the information only in connection with continuing discussions by the parties concerning the Transactions, except as may otherwise be mutually agreed upon in writing, and shall reproduce such information only to the extent necessary for such purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: With respect to materials disclosed under this Agreement, the party to whom the information is disclosed and its Representatives shall: a. hold the information in confidence, exercising a degree of care not less than the care used by such party to protect its own proprietary or confidential information that it does not wish to disclose, and in no event less than a reasonable degree of care; b. restrict disclosure of the information solely to those Representatives with a need to know and not disclose it to any other person; c. advise those Representatives of their obligations with respect to the information; and d. use the information only in connection with continuing discussions by the parties concerning the Transactions, except as may otherwise be mutually agreed upon in writing, and shall reproduce such information only to the extent necessary for such purpose.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'information' to 'materials' in the first sentence creates an in-text contradiction. This clashes with the definition of 'Information' defined above, which the rest of the clauses still use to refer.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In connection with ongoing discussions between Broker and Company concerning possible transactions (the "Transactions"), each party to this Agreement may wish to disclose certain proprietary and confidential information to the other party on a confidential basis. Such proprietary or confidential information ("Information") includes any and all technical and non-technical information, including without limitation, information concerning financial, accounting or marketing reports, business plans, analysis, forecasts, predictors, intellectual property, trade secrets and know-how disclosed in connection with the Transactions. "Information" may take the form of documentation, drawings, specifications, software, technical or engineering data and other forms, and may be communicated orally, in writing, by electronic or magnetic media, by visual observation and by other means. "Information" includes any reports, analysis, studies or other material, whether prepared by the receiving party or otherwise, that contains or are based upon proprietary or confidential information covered by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses the term 'information' which is explicitly defined in the 'Contradicted Text'. The changed text replaces 'information' with 'materials', but the rest of the clause still uses 'information'. This creates a contradiction because the defined 'Information' might not be the same as 'materials' leading to ambiguity about what is covered under the confidentiality obligations. The change therefore breaks compatibility with the contradicted section, making enforcement unclear.

perturbation.original_text: Information shall be deemed the property of the disclosing Party and, within ten (10) business days upon written request from the disclosing party, the other party will return all such information received in tangible form to the disclosing party or will destroy all such information.
perturbation.changed_text: Data shall be deemed the property of the disclosing Party and, within ten (10) business days upon written request from the disclosing party, the other party will return all such information received in tangible form to the disclosing party or will destroy all such information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Information' to 'Data' at the beginning of the sentence. Throughout the entire section it refers to 'information' and in that same sentence, it refers to information making it more confusing on if there is a difference between 'Data' and 'information' that the parties needs to be awared of.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Information shall be deemed the property of the disclosing Party and, within ten (10) business days upon written request from the disclosing party, the other party will return all such information received in tangible form to the disclosing party or will destroy all such information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Information' with 'Data' at the start of a sentence while the rest of the section, including the contradicted text, refers to 'information'. This creates a 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' as defined: 'Misaligned terminology also leads to in-text contradictions when the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations'. It introduces potential confusion and uncertainty regarding whether 'Data' and 'information' are distinct terms with different implications.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that the receiving party or its employees or Representatives (a) need (for securities law purposes) to make disclosures of information or (b) are required by law, regulations, or government agency or court orders, interrogatories, requests for information or documents, subpoenas, or civil investigative demands to disclose any information, in the case of (a) the receiving party shall provide the disclosing party with prompt written notice so that the disclosing party can work with the receiving party to limit the disclosure to the greatest extent possible consistent with legal obligations (it being understood that disclosure of the name of the other party will never be made without that party's prior written consent); or in he case of (b) the receiving party shall use its reasonable efforts to minimize such disclosure and obtain an assurance that the recipient shall accord confidential treatment to the information, and shall notify the disclosing party contemporaneously of such disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that the receiving party or its employees or Representatives (a) need (for securities law purposes) to make disclosures of confidential data or (b) are required by law, regulations, or government agency or court orders, interrogatories, requests for confidential data or documents, subpoenas, or civil investigative demands to disclose any data, in the case of (a) the receiving party shall provide the disclosing party with prompt written notice so that the disclosing party can work with the receiving party to limit the disclosure to the greatest extent possible consistent with legal obligations (it being understood that disclosure of the name of the other party will never be made without that party's prior written consent); or in he case of (b) the receiving party shall use its reasonable efforts to minimize such disclosure and obtain an assurance that the recipient shall accord confidential treatment to the data, and shall notify the disclosing party contemporaneously of such disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'information' to 'confidential data' and 'data' in section 10, creates in-text contradiction, it doesn't say that 'information' and 'data' or 'confidential data' are the same. It's uncertain what 'data' is referring to now because it has not been specified. Previously it just said 'information', meaning the defined term can be extracted out. Now there is uncertainity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that the receiving party or its employees or Representatives (a) need (for securities law purposes) to make disclosures of information or (b) are required by law, regulations, or government agency or court orders, interrogatories, requests for information or documents, subpoenas, or civil investigative demands to disclose any information, in the case of (a) the receiving party shall provide the disclosing party with prompt written notice so that the disclosing party can work with the receiving party to limit the disclosure to the greatest extent possible consistent with legal obligations (it being understood that disclosure of the name of the other party will never be made without that party's prior written consent); or in he case of (b) the receiving party shall use its reasonable efforts to minimize such disclosure and obtain an assurance that the recipient shall accord confidential treatment to the information, and shall notify the disclosing party contemporaneously of such disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'information' to 'confidential data' and 'data' creates an in-text contradiction because the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations, this falls under 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'. The contradicted text uses 'information' in reference to disclosures, while the changed text uses 'confidential data' and 'data', creating uncertainty about whether all 'information' is considered 'confidential data' or 'data', or if 'data' encompasses a broader set of information than 'confidential data'. This ambiguity creates a conflict within the document.
